input:
READMISSION SHEET
This is the child BENAMEUR SID AHMED aged 08, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a DIP type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for the 6th venoglobulin treatment. Reason for hospitalization: 6th course of venoglobulin. HISTORY:
Family:
Father: 46 years old, BES. Mother: 34 years old, BES. Siblings: daughter aged 09 with asthma, screening in progress
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: yes
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: at term by upper route
Apgar 10/10Neonatal resuscitation: no
Birth weight: 3.7kg
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: mixed from birth. Vaccination: correctly vaccinated
No delay in psychomotor development
Nursery: no
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Diversification at the age of 5 months
Vitamin D received at the age of 01 months/06 months. Pathological:
Hospitalization in the EHS CANASTEL infectious disease department on 06/02/2025 for the case of a prolonged fever attributed to an EBV infection
2nd hospitalization on 04/2025 in the same department for a return of fever with a rash
Environment:
Habitat: not humid
No animals
No passive smoking
History of the disease:
Date of diagnosis: 04/22/2025
Age of diagnosis: 8 years
Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Prolonged fever
Atypical febrile rash
Paraclinic:
Active replication of EBV: EBV viral load positive 3.3 log, aevc IgG EBNA and VCA +, IgM neg
Specific dosage of serum proteins on 04/22/2025 at LILLE hospital:
IgM 0.32 g/L
IgA 0.55
IgG 3.5 g/L
Immunodeficiency assessment done on 13/05/2025 in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS:
Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia associated with a virtual absence of B lymphocytes. cellular immunity is preserved. 2nd assessment of immunodeficiency carried out on 22/06/2025 in BENI MESOUS ALGIERS: confirms the diagnosis: common variable immunodeficiency. Hospitalization histories:
PROCESSING IN PROGRESS:
Bactrim (40/200) at preventive dose
EVENTS SINCE LAST PASSAGE:
No notion of fever or rash
No respiratory or ENT damage
No hospitalizations
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 23kg100 (-0.85 DS): Weight gain of 300g
Size: 127cm(-0.23DS)
BMI: 14.3(-1.14DS)
Gomez score: 88%
Waterloo Score: 89%
Growth curve:
The child shows harmonious growth (proportional height and weight) and no delay in weight stature. General condition: preserved, T° 36.7 c, TCNC, dextro: 1.06 g/l
Skin examination: CV (BCG) present, TCNC, no skin lesion
ENT examination: no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 25 c/min
	No signs of respiratory distress. SPO2: 98% AA
Pulmonary auscultation: clear at 2CP
no signs of chronic respiratory insufficiency (finger clubbing), no thoracic deformity. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible no murmurs or BSA
Digestive examination: soft abdomen no organomegaly, transit preserved: 1 stool/day with normal appearance. Urogenital examination: Male morphotype, no abnormalities of sexual differentiation, Tanner stage 1
Review of other devices: is unremarkable
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. VVP. Pre-therapeutic assessment. A course of immunoglobulin: dose of 0.8 mg/kg/4h. Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Evolution:
The treatment passes without incident
The patient leaves after the end of the treatment with an appointment for his next venoglobulin treatment on 11/23/2025
CONCLUSION
This is the child BENAMEUR SID AHMED aged 08, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a PID type common variable immunodeficiency admitted to our service for a 6th course of venoglobulin passed without incident. Exams requested:
- Request for total IgG
- FNS request, viral serology for the sister as part of the screening in order to launch the immune assessment.
